_note: RECORDED real Claude layout proposal for the WT/KO fixture — replayed by tests/test_layout_cassette.py. Do not hand-edit; re-capture with scripts/capture_layout_cassette.py.

input.plain_summary: All four files share the same naming scheme `<CONDITION>_<N>.h5ad` and have no obs columns of their own, so every semantic label must come from a proposed constant. The leading token (WT / KO) reads as the experimental condition: two wild-type shards and two knockout shards. The trailing number reads as a biological replicate index within each condition (WT_1, WT_2, KO_1, KO_2). I've marked condition as high confidence (clear, consistent filename token) and replicate as medium (the trailing integer plausibly indexes a replicate, but it could instead denote a batch, lane, or re-sequencing run — a human should confirm which unit it represents). I did not propose `batch` because nothing in the metadata distinguishes a batch from the replicate index. `sample_id` is handled deterministically and is not proposed here.
unresolved: batch: no metadata token or shared obs column distinguishes a batch grouping from the replicate index
whether the trailing number is a biological replicate vs. a technical batch/lane/run — human should confirm the unit
